title: [lichen_l2.c:693] secure_zero logs eui64 after zeroing
description: In lichen_peer_remove(), the LOG_INF on line 693 logs the eui64 address, but secure_zero(entry->eui64) was already called on line 691. This logs all zeros instead of the actual removed peer address. Fix: either log BEFORE zeroing, or copy the eui64 to a local variable first.
close_reason: Invalid: The LOG_INF uses the function parameter 'eui64' (caller-provided), not 'entry->eui64' (table entry). These are separate memory locations - peer_find_locked compares them but returns a pointer to the table entry, not the parameter. The parameter remains intact after entry->eui64 is zeroed.
